title: Parallel simulation builds with concatenated .cpp files (v2)
body:
Improved version of https://github.com/verilator/verilator/pull/4642

## Usage

Add `--output-groups N` to verilator command line. It is recommended to set `N` to value higher than number of parallel jobs, e.g. 2-4 times more. Note that this feature does nothing when number of output files is smaller than 100. This is mainly useful on project with huge amount of output files, counted in thousands.

For stats and debug information add `--debugi-V3EmitMk 6`.  


## Changes from [previous version](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/pull/4642)

- The file grouping implementation has been moved to V3EmitMk.cpp.
  
- Each file score is based on code fragments writted to it, not on the file size as it was before.
  The score is incremented using `increaseComplexityScore()` and `increaseTypesFileComplexityScore()` functions called in the same places where the code fragment is emitted.
  I find it convenient, as it clearly links score increments with C++ code that causes it.
  Currently the scores increments for each fragment are arbitrary, but they work quite well. This always can be tuned later.
  
- Large files are excluded from concatenation. Threshold is simply calculated with `totalScore / bucketsNum / 2`, where `2` is arbitrary.
  
  - This one could be improved, but I don't have any ideas at this moment. When merged, I'll probably look how it works with larger designs I'll be working with in the future.

  - Initially I've tried using threshold based on standard deviation (* 3), but it resulted in large number of work lists (with UVM),
    which in turn resulted in long list of output files and noticeably longer build times.
  
- The issue with change of the files order when a DepSet hash changes has been not adressed.
  A file changes its position on the list only when its dependencies change, am I right? The file list is not sorted by name nor hash.
  If that's true the file order should be resistant to many (most?) changes made during development.
  
- The command line option is named `--output-groups`. The main reason is that the option has little to do with jobs.
  It can be beneficial to set it to higher value than number of parallel jobs, e.g. 2x more. This results in smaller groups which can be queued more efficiently.
  
- There's a lot of debug logging in the grouping function that I used during development. Enabled using `--debugi-V3EmitMk 6`
  I've kept it as I think it could be quite useful in improving the algorithm or when tuning the scores.
  Few things that are printed to the log:
  
  - Score assigned to each file
  
  - Scores histogram and calculated threshold score used for exclusion of large files
  
  - Work Lists, their total score, and whether they are concatenable
  
  - How many buckets have been assigned to each work list

  - List of final output groups and files

- There is a script for generating list of fake files with user-defined scores (`nodist/gen_scores_list_for_concatenation_test`),
  and an option in Verilator (`--debug-test-concatenation FILE_LIST`) that allows reading such list and passing it through concatenation function.
  Used for regression test, but also useful during algorithm modification or scoring changes.
